steps.title: Spot the range overlap
steps.content: The passage establishes that individual amphibian studies showed differences well above the average for mammals, even though mammal studies showed larger average differences overall. This reveals that the ranges of effects overlap.

steps.title: Apply the overlap to the choices
steps.content: Since individual amphibian studies exceeded the mammal average, some amphibian studies must have found larger effects than some mammal studies—exactly what choice B concludes. Choices A, C, and D either contradict the stated trend, lack textual support, or reference information not discussed in the passage. That matches choice B — B is correct.
